hero.kicker: find the pattern in your poetry
hero.title: Discover your Poet Personality
hero.subtitle: Your poems reveal more than style. They reveal how you feel, what you notice, and how you turn experience into language.
hero.body: The Versiq Analyzer studies your writing across imagery, emotional tone, themes, structure, rhythm, and voice to identify your poetic type. The more poems you submit, the more accurate your profile becomes.

proofStrip: Pattern-based analysis across your submitted poems

howItWorks.title: How the Analyzer Works
howItWorks.intro: Your Versiq is based on recurring patterns in your writing over time.

steps.title: Submit your poems
steps.body: Start with one poem. Add more over time to improve precision.

steps.title: Detect recurring signals
steps.body: The analyzer looks across emotional tone, imagery and symbolism, rhythm and sound, structure and lineation, vulnerability, recurring themes, narrative vs impressionistic tendencies, simplicity vs density, and directness vs ambiguity.

steps.title: Map your poetic center
steps.body: You receive a primary type, adjacent traits, and profile-strength level.

steps.title: Refine your profile
steps.body: One poem can reflect a mood. Several poems reveal a pattern.

groups.name: Visionaries
groups.description: Driven by intuition, meaning, depth, and discovery. They interpret the unseen and pursue truths that cannot be reached directly.

groups.name: Romantics
groups.description: Guided by beauty, intimacy, devotion, and emotional atmosphere. Their work centers on feeling deeply and expressing that depth with resonance.

groups.name: Truth-Tellers
groups.description: Defined by honesty, realism, and emotional courage. They face what hurts, what matters, and what deserves to be said.

groups.name: Makers
groups.description: Shaped by craft, form, delivery, and design. They build poems with structural intention and compositional control.

types.name: The Alchemist
types.shortBlurb: Turns pain, memory, and longing into transformation.
types.subtitle: The poet who makes beauty from what burns.
types.overview: The Alchemist turns feeling into transformation. Their poems often process pain, desire, memory, and change into language that feels intimate and redemptive.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: Readers who connect with The Alchemist might resonate with Rumi, Sylvia Plath, and Seamus Heaney.

body: The Alchemist writes from rupture, then finds language that turns wound into wisdom.
Your best work doesn’t deny darkness—it reworks it into something strangely luminous.

list: Symbolic language that evolves across a poem
Endings that feel earned, not decorative
body: You rarely leave a feeling untouched; you transform it.

sections.title: What defines The Alchemist
body: The Alchemist is defined by emotional transmutation: turning rupture into meaning without diluting intensity.
This type is less interested in polished distance and more interested in earned transformation.

sections.title: Core Alchemist traits
list: Symbolic language that evolves across a poem
Endings that feel earned, not decorative

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Alchemist poems often begin inside conflict and move toward reframed agency.
Fire, body, and renewal imagery frequently carry the emotional arc.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, intensity creates healing clarity.
Overextended, intensity becomes dense and obscures the central line of feeling.
At your strongest, readers feel accompanied through difficult emotional terrain.
Your shadow is over-saturation—too much intensity can blur the poem’s center.

sections.title: The most prominent The Alchemist strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Alchemist can spiral into emotional over-processing and symbolic overload.
The signal is intensity without integration: everything feels meaningful, but nothing fully resolves.

sections.title: What motivates The Alchemist
body: The Alchemist is most motivated by transformation—turning rupture into language that heals, clarifies, or liberates.
Creative momentum grows when there is honest risk and a path toward emotional renewal.

sections.title: How to grow as The Alchemist
body: Set formal constraints when emotion spikes (line count, stanza limits, concrete image quotas).
Aim for one clean emotional thesis per poem before layering metaphors.

writingStyle.intro: Your lines arc from rupture to renewal.
list: Metaphors of fire, ash, tide, skin, metal
body: Your structure often mirrors change itself: fracture, recognition, renewal.

sections.title: How The Alchemist writes
body: The Alchemist writes in arcs: wound, pressure, change, integration.
Line-level choices usually intensify then resolve rather than remain static.

sections.title: Signature writing signals
list: Metaphors of fire, ash, tide, skin, metal

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence often tightens around pain, then opens toward insight.
The voice is intimate, high-stakes, and redemptive without sentimentality.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Keep the heat, but trim duplicate metaphors that repeat the same emotional move.
Strengthen closure by naming the shift, not just implying it.

loveRelationships.intro: In intimacy, you seek transformation with trust.
body: In intimacy, you seek bonds that can survive truth, loss, and reinvention.
When insecure, you may test devotion through intensity; when grounded, you offer profound emotional healing.

types.name: The Oracle
types.shortBlurb: Writes in symbols, signs, and hidden truths.
types.subtitle: The poet who senses what lies beneath the surface.
types.overview: The Oracle writes as if truth arrives in flashes. Their work is intuitive, symbolic, and suggestive rather than explicit.
types.idealTagline: You write what cannot be said directly.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type often feels close in spirit to Emily Dickinson, Rumi, and T. S. Eliot.

overview.intro: You speak through symbol before statement.
body: The Oracle doesn’t explain everything; it reveals in flashes.
Your poems feel like messages half-heard in a dream and remembered as truth.
You write the unsayable by circling it with charged images.

sections.title: What defines The Oracle
body: The Oracle is defined by intuitive pattern-recognition and symbolic speech.
Meaning arrives through charged implication rather than direct declaration.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Oracle poems frequently operate through echoes, omens, and dream logic.
Readers feel the truth before they can paraphrase it.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, ambiguity creates depth.
Overextended, ambiguity becomes opacity and emotional distance.
At your strongest, readers feel mysteriously understood.
Your shadow is obscurity—too much veil can weaken emotional access.

sections.title: The most prominent The Oracle strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Oracle may retreat too far into ambiguity and become inaccessible even to themselves.
The signal is mystique replacing meaning: atmosphere remains, center blurs.

sections.title: What motivates The Oracle
body: The Oracle is motivated by revelation—capturing what can be sensed before it can be explained.
They thrive when symbols remain charged but emotionally legible.

sections.title: How to grow as The Oracle
body: Anchor each symbolic poem with one concrete human moment or scene.
During revision, test every abstraction against clarity: “What does this image do emotionally?”

writingStyle.intro: Your poems imply first, then reveal.
body: You build resonance through omission, echo, and motif recurrence.

sections.title: How The Oracle writes
body: The Oracle writes through suggestion, atmosphere, and layered symbol.
The style favors resonance over explanation.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Lines tend to drift, loop, and reveal by accretion.
The voice feels prophetic, private, and quietly exacting.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Anchor symbolic passages with one concrete human detail.
Cut any image that is mysterious but emotionally nonfunctional.

loveRelationships.intro: In relationships, you read subtext instinctively.
body: In love, you often sense shifts before they are spoken.
When regulated, you offer rare emotional attunement; when overwhelmed, you can retreat into silence or riddles.

types.name: The Architect
types.shortBlurb: Crafts poems with precision, structure, and control.
types.subtitle: The poet who builds with intention.
types.overview: The Architect values form. Their poems are deliberate, composed, and technically disciplined even when emotionally charged.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: People drawn to this type might also admire Elizabeth Bishop, Robert Frost, and T. S. Eliot.

body: The Architect values design: line, turn, proportion, restraint.
Even your most vulnerable poems feel intentionally engineered.

coreTraits.intro: Design is how your feeling thinks.
body: You believe form is not decoration—it is meaning.

sections.title: What defines The Architect
body: The Architect is defined by structural intelligence and technical composure.
Emotion is shaped through form rather than spilled around it.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Architect poems feel intentionally built, with clear internal scaffolding.
Even vulnerable passages maintain formal integrity.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, structure amplifies feeling.
Overextended, control can flatten spontaneity and warmth.
At your best, your work is elegant, durable, and rereadable.
Your shadow is over-management—too much polish can mute spontaneity.

sections.title: The most prominent The Architect strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Architect over-controls the work and polishes away vulnerability.
The signal is impeccable structure with muted emotional voltage.

sections.title: What motivates The Architect
body: The Architect is motivated by elegant precision—where form and feeling become inseparable.
They are energized by hard craft problems and clean, deliberate execution.

sections.title: How to grow as The Architect
body: Draft one “messy pass” before technical revision to preserve raw signal.
Trade perfection for presence by leaving one intentional rough edge per poem.
Your poems often reveal an internal blueprint readers can feel, even if they cannot name it.

sections.title: How The Architect writes
body: The Architect writes like a designer: proportion, sequence, and precision lead.
Form choices are semantic, not decorative.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence is deliberate and measured, with clean turns.
The voice reads as composed, intelligent, and exact.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Keep one line where emotion arrives less managed.
Prioritize vividness alongside precision in late-stage edits.

loveRelationships.intro: In love, reliability and design matter to you.
body: In relationships, you show care through steadiness, consistency, and thoughtful action.
Under stress, you may default to control; in trust, you become deeply reliable and emotionally articulate.

types.name: The Seeker
types.shortBlurb: Writes to search, question, and understand.
types.subtitle: The poet always moving toward something deeper.
types.overview: The Seeker writes to discover. Their poems are driven by questions, longing, and the feeling that meaning is near but not fully grasped.
types.idealTagline: You write to find what matters.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type may resonate with Walt Whitman, John Keats, and Seamus Heaney.

overview.intro: You write to discover what is true.
body: The Seeker is animated by questions, thresholds, and becoming.
Your poems feel like journeys where insight arrives mid-walk, not at the starting line.
You are less interested in final answers than honest pursuit.

sections.title: What defines The Seeker
body: The Seeker is defined by inquiry, longing, and movement toward meaning.
This type writes from the threshold between uncertainty and insight.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Seeker poems often progress through searching turns rather than fixed claims.
Questions function as engines, not ornaments.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, curiosity generates emotional depth.
Overextended, searching delays arrival and weakens impact.
At your strongest, readers trust your searching mind.
Your shadow is perpetual incompletion—circling can replace arriving.

sections.title: The most prominent The Seeker strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Seeker can stay in inquiry mode indefinitely and avoid committing to meaning.
The signal is beautiful searching with weak arrival.

sections.title: What motivates The Seeker
body: The Seeker is motivated by discovery—moving from uncertainty toward earned understanding.
They are energized by poems that feel like real intellectual and emotional journeys.

sections.title: How to grow as The Seeker
body: Define the question early, then require one concrete answer by the final section.
Pair philosophical movement with sensory specifics to prevent drift.

writingStyle.intro: Your syntax moves like thought in pursuit.
body: Your strongest poems pair curiosity with concrete emotional stakes.

sections.title: How The Seeker writes
body: The Seeker writes in motion, letting thought evolve on the page.
Meaning accumulates through steps rather than instant conclusions.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence leans exploratory, with reflective pivots.
The voice is earnest, probing, and philosophically alive.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Ensure at least one concrete arrival by the ending.
Balance abstract inquiry with sensory stakes.

loveRelationships.intro: In partnership, growth matters as much as closeness.
body: In connection, you value growth, reflection, and meaningful conversation.
When uncertain, you may keep seeking instead of settling; when centered, you bring depth, wonder, and evolution to partnership.

types.name: The Lover
types.shortBlurb: Led by intimacy, desire, and emotional connection.
types.subtitle: The poet who writes from the heart outward.
types.overview: The Lover centers intimacy. Their poems are tender, desirous, and emotionally immediate, often shaped by devotion, longing, and memory.
types.idealTagline: You write to touch and be touched.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: Readers who identify with this type might feel drawn to Pablo Neruda, Sappho, and John Keats.

overview.intro: You write from contact, ache, and closeness.
body: The Lover privileges intimacy: the body remembered, the voice missed, the hand almost reached.
Your poems translate attachment into immediate emotional presence.

coreTraits.intro: Attachment is your poetic center of gravity.
list: Themes of longing, devotion, and absence
body: You make readers feel the distance between people—and the desire to cross it.

sections.title: What defines The Lover
body: The Lover is defined by relational intensity and emotional immediacy.
Poems are driven by contact, desire, and vulnerability.

sections.title: Core Lover traits
list: Themes of longing, devotion, and absence

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Lover poems often center touch, memory, and emotional proximity.
Second-person address is common and often effective.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, intimacy feels generous and vivid.
Overextended, intensity can become melodramatic or idealized.
At your best, your warmth is unforgettable and deeply human.
Your shadow is romantic over-identification—feeling can outrun proportion.

sections.title: The most prominent The Lover strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Lover may over-identify with longing and lose proportion.
The signal is emotional saturation that narrows perspective.

sections.title: What motivates The Lover
body: The Lover is motivated by intimacy—language that creates contact, tenderness, and relational truth.
They thrive when poems feel both vulnerable and reciprocal.

sections.title: How to grow as The Lover
body: Balance desire-driven lines with observational lines grounded in reality.
Write one poem from your own perspective and one from the other person’s.

writingStyle.intro: Your diction is tactile and direct.
body: You create closeness quickly, often from the first line.

sections.title: How The Lover writes
body: The Lover writes from the body outward, with high emotional contact.
Language often privileges closeness over conceptual distance.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence tends to be flowing, declarative, and warm.
The voice invites immediate emotional participation.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Preserve tenderness while reducing repetition of longing statements.
Counterbalance declarations with concrete scene detail.

loveRelationships.intro: In love, you reach for closeness without armor.
body: You seek reciprocity, emotional presence, and sincere affection.
When hurt, you may idealize or cling; when secure, you become generous, grounding, and profoundly affectionate.

types.name: The Dreamer
types.shortBlurb: Lives in atmosphere, wonder, and emotional possibility.
types.subtitle: The poet who drifts between reality and imagination.
types.overview: The Dreamer writes in atmosphere. Their poems often feel luminous, wistful, and emotionally suspended between memory and imagination.
types.idealTagline: You write what longing looks like in the air.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type often shares a similar energy with John Keats, Bashō, and Emily Dickinson.

overview.intro: You write in atmosphere before argument.
body: The Dreamer lives in the soft threshold between memory and imagination.
Your poems feel suspended—like light in dust, like a room after rain.
You compose emotional weather as much as narrative content.

sections.title: What defines The Dreamer
body: The Dreamer is defined by atmosphere, imagination, and emotional light-play.
Mood often carries the meaning as much as narrative does.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Dreamer poems often glide between memory and possibility.
Transitions feel intuitive, like weather patterns.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, atmosphere becomes transportive.
Overextended, atmosphere can turn vague and ungrounded.
At your strongest, readers are carried into a living interior landscape.
Your shadow is diffusion—too much haze can thin emotional stakes.

sections.title: The most prominent The Dreamer strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Dreamer can dissolve into mood and avoid emotional confrontation.
The signal is beautiful atmosphere with low narrative or thematic traction.

sections.title: What motivates The Dreamer
body: The Dreamer is motivated by wonder—capturing emotional weather with grace and originality.
They are energized when imagination remains tethered to felt human stakes.

sections.title: How to grow as The Dreamer
body: For every abstract image, add one concrete object or action.
In revision, identify one sentence that names what is truly at risk.

writingStyle.intro: Your poems drift with deliberate control.
body: Your best work balances lyric mist with one clear human anchor.

sections.title: How The Dreamer writes
body: The Dreamer writes in soft focus with deliberate tonal cohesion.
Imagery is often airy, sensory, and wistful.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence is gentle and suspended, with emotional drift.
The voice feels wistful, imaginative, and tenderly cinematic.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Add one grounding image per stanza to preserve clarity.
Sharpen one emotional thesis line near the ending.

loveRelationships.intro: In relationships, emotional weather matters deeply.
body: You are drawn to emotional possibility, tenderness, and shared reverie.
When ungrounded, projection can replace presence; when grounded, you offer romance with real emotional care.

types.name: The Muse
types.shortBlurb: Drawn to beauty, elegance, and emotional allure.
types.subtitle: The poet who makes language feel magnetic.
types.overview: The Muse prioritizes beauty and tonal elegance. Their writing is often lyrical, polished, and emotionally attractive from the first line.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: People close to this type may admire Sappho, Pablo Neruda, and Mary Oliver.

body: The Muse makes language seductive without sacrificing coherence.
Your poems often arrive polished, magnetic, and quotable.
You understand that style can be substance when handled with precision.

sections.title: What defines The Muse
body: The Muse is defined by aesthetic fluency and tonal elegance.
Beauty is treated as a serious craft objective, not just decoration.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Muse poems often feel quotable, polished, and sonically controlled.
Visual and verbal allure are central to effect.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, beauty amplifies emotional precision.
Overextended, style can outpace vulnerability.

strengthsShadows.intro: You make beauty memorable, not fragile.
body: At your best, your lines linger in memory and conversation.
Your shadow is surface over core—ornament can overtake emotional risk.

sections.title: The most prominent The Muse strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Muse can prioritize polish over depth and perform refinement.
The signal is gorgeous language that does not fully open.

sections.title: What motivates The Muse
body: The Muse is motivated by aesthetic mastery—making beauty memorable, articulate, and alive.
They thrive when elegance carries genuine emotional consequence.

sections.title: How to grow as The Muse
body: Interrogate your prettiest lines: keep only those that advance meaning.
Introduce one vulnerable detail that cannot be stylized away.

writingStyle.intro: Your line work is polished and musical.
body: You write with a keen ear for line beauty and tonal consistency.

sections.title: How The Muse writes
body: The Muse writes with lyrical control and high line-finish.
Every phrase is selected for feel, texture, and memorability.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence is poised and melodic, often with graceful lift.
The voice is magnetic, refined, and emotionally curated.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Challenge your most polished line: does it deepen meaning or only decorate?
Introduce one unvarnished detail to increase emotional trust.

loveRelationships.intro: In intimacy, admiration and presence go together.
body: In intimacy, you notice details and cultivate moments that feel artful and meaningful.
When insecure, performance can replace vulnerability; when open, you become deeply affectionate and emotionally elegant.

types.name: The Devotee
types.shortBlurb: Writes with sincerity, reverence, and care.
types.subtitle: The poet who honors what matters.
types.overview: The Devotee writes with reverence. Their poems return to what deserves care: memory, love, faith, grief, ritual, and place.
types.idealTagline: You write with devotion, not distance.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type might resonate with Mary Oliver, Rumi, and Walt Whitman.

overview.intro: You write with reverence for what matters.
body: The Devotee returns to love, memory, place, ritual, and sacred attention.
Your poems feel like offerings: careful, sincere, and emotionally clean.
You treat subjects with care rather than spectacle.

sections.title: What defines The Devotee
body: The Devotee is defined by reverence, steadiness, and moral tenderness.
This type writes to honor what should be held with care.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Devotee poems often return to ritual, memory, and commitment.
Repetition functions as devotion, not redundancy.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, sincerity feels grounding and profound.
Overextended, tone can narrow into earnest sameness.
At your strongest, your poems restore trust in tenderness.
Your shadow is over-earnestness—irony and play may disappear.

sections.title: The most prominent The Devotee strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Devotee can become overly earnest and emotionally singular.
The signal is sincerity without enough tonal variation.

sections.title: What motivates The Devotee
body: The Devotee is motivated by reverence—honoring people, places, and values through careful language.
They are energized by writing that feels faithful, grounded, and morally coherent.

sections.title: How to grow as The Devotee
body: Practice tonal contrast: pair tenderness with tension or irony in revision.
Expand range by writing beyond familiar sacred subjects once per cycle.

writingStyle.intro: Your pacing is steady, ceremonial, and warm.
list: Grounded imagery of home, body, earth
body: You create continuity through recurring motifs and moral warmth.

sections.title: How The Devotee writes
body: The Devotee writes with measured cadence and sacred attention.
Language tends to be plain-spoken, warm, and deliberate.

sections.title: Signature writing signals
list: Grounded imagery of home, body, earth

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence is steady and prayer-like, with low ego pressure.
The voice feels faithful, intimate, and trustworthy.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Preserve sincerity while adding tonal contrast where needed.
Use one disruptive image to prevent devotional flattening.

loveRelationships.intro: In love, loyalty is a daily practice.
body: You value commitment, consistency, and shared meaning.
When strained, you may over-give; when balanced, you create relationships that feel safe, honored, and enduring.

types.name: The Confessor
types.shortBlurb: Speaks with raw honesty and emotional exposure.
types.subtitle: The poet who says what others avoid.
types.overview: The Confessor writes from exposure rather than distance. Their poems are direct, vulnerable, and emotionally fearless.
types.idealTagline: You write the truth before it is comfortable.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: Readers drawn here might resonate with Sylvia Plath, Anne Sexton, and Maya Angelou.

overview.intro: You write what others avoid saying aloud.
body: The Confessor is driven by raw truth, personal risk, and emotional disclosure.
Your work often reads like necessity rather than performance.

coreTraits.intro: Truth arrives unmasked in your lines.
body: You use poetry as an act of honest exposure.

sections.title: What defines The Confessor
body: The Confessor is defined by emotional exposure and uncompromised honesty.
Poetry here is a truth-act, not a distancing exercise.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Confessor poems often center disclosure, shame, desire, and lived contradiction.
The force comes from saying what is usually withheld.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, vulnerability creates profound connection.
Overextended, disclosure can exceed craft containment.
At your best, readers feel less alone in their own complexity.
Your shadow is emotional flooding—confession can outrun shaping.

sections.title: The most prominent The Confessor strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Confessor may move from honest disclosure into emotional flooding.
The signal is raw truth delivered without sufficient shaping distance.

sections.title: What motivates The Confessor
body: The Confessor is motivated by liberation through truth-telling.
They thrive when vulnerability is both brave and crafted.

sections.title: How to grow as The Confessor
body: Separate drafting from publishing—write raw, then revise for structure and reader containment.
Use scene and image to hold intensity instead of repeating declarations.

writingStyle.intro: Your delivery is intimate, stripped, and immediate.
body: Your strongest pieces pair vulnerability with deliberate craft.

sections.title: How The Confessor writes
body: The Confessor writes in direct, high-voltage first person.
Language favors candor and immediacy over ornament.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence often spikes with urgency, then softens into consequence.
The voice is raw, intimate, and confrontationally honest.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Keep the truth, then shape it for reader stamina and clarity.
Convert repeated confession into scene, image, and structural turn.

loveRelationships.intro: In connection, truth is your first language.
body: In relationships, you want realness over politeness.
When dysregulated, intensity can overwhelm; when grounded, you offer extraordinary honesty and emotional loyalty.

types.name: The Witness
types.shortBlurb: Observes the world with quiet depth and realism.
types.subtitle: The poet who notices what others miss.
types.overview: The Witness writes through attention. Their poems preserve ordinary detail, social texture, and understated human truth.
types.idealTagline: You make the ordinary impossible to ignore.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type often feels close to Elizabeth Bishop, Langston Hughes, and Seamus Heaney.

overview.intro: You write by paying close attention.
body: The Witness preserves what is easily overlooked: gestures, streets, weather, ordinary grief.
Your poems build meaning through precise observation.
You trust evidence on the page more than abstraction.

sections.title: What defines The Witness
body: The Witness is defined by observational precision and humane realism.
Meaning is built through what is seen, heard, and faithfully recorded.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Witness poems foreground places, objects, gestures, and social texture.
Emotion emerges through scene pressure rather than overt declaration.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, restraint deepens impact.
Overextended, restraint can read as detachment.
At your strongest, your restraint creates deep emotional impact.
Your shadow is distance—recording can eclipse revelation.

sections.title: The most prominent The Witness strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Witness can over-document and under-interpret.
The signal is excellent observation with restrained emotional revelation.

sections.title: What motivates The Witness
body: The Witness is motivated by accuracy and dignity—showing life as it is without distortion.
They are energized by precise detail that quietly carries moral weight.

sections.title: How to grow as The Witness
body: After each observational stanza, add one interpretive or emotional turn.
Allow selective subjectivity: what did this scene change in you?

writingStyle.intro: Your scenes do the emotional speaking.
body: You reveal feeling by showing the world clearly.

sections.title: How The Witness writes
body: The Witness writes through detail-rich scenes and measured distance.
The style privileges specificity over abstraction.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence is grounded, observant, and quietly purposeful.
The voice feels attentive, ethical, and clear-eyed.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: After each descriptive sequence, add one interpretive or emotional inflection.
Trim excess cataloging that does not alter stakes.

loveRelationships.intro: In partnership, you show love by noticing.
body: You show care by remembering details and honoring reality as it is.
When guarded, you may understate need; when open, you become a deeply dependable and emotionally perceptive partner.

types.name: The Rebel
types.subtitle: The poet who writes against what feels dead.
types.overview: The Rebel resists the safe version. Their poems push against falsehood, inherited rules, and emotional or political silence.
types.idealTagline: You would rather disrupt than pretend.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: People drawn to this type might admire Allen Ginsberg, Audre Lorde, and Adrienne Rich.

overview.intro: You write against numbness and pretense.
body: The Rebel is oppositional in service of truth, life, and freedom.
Your poems push form, language, and expectation to create real contact.
You reject dead language and inherited scripts.

sections.title: What defines The Rebel
body: The Rebel is defined by refusal—against dead language, falsehood, and imposed limits.
Its best work converts resistance into aesthetic force.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Rebel poems interrupt expectations through diction, structure, and stance.
Energy is oppositional but purposeful when healthy.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, disruption creates liberation and insight.
Overextended, defiance can become reaction without depth.

strengthsShadows.intro: You jolt language back to life.
body: At your best, your work wakes readers up.
Your shadow is reaction without integration—defiance can flatten complexity.

sections.title: The most prominent The Rebel strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Rebel can become reactive and flatten complexity into opposition.
The signal is forceful stance with reduced nuance.

sections.title: What motivates The Rebel
body: The Rebel is motivated by disruption in service of truth.
They thrive when energy is directed, principled, and artistically controlled.

sections.title: How to grow as The Rebel
body: Channel urgency into form: design escalation rather than constant volume.
Interrogate enemies and allies with equal rigor to preserve credibility.

writingStyle.intro: Your form prefers voltage and fracture.
body: Your strongest poems channel anger into shape, not noise.

sections.title: How The Rebel writes
body: The Rebel writes with voltage, fracture, and refusal of polite cadence.
Form often carries argument through rupture.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence is percussive, volatile, and insistently present.
The voice is bold, anti-false, and morally confrontational.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Keep force, but vary intensity to preserve nuance.
Strengthen credibility by interrogating your own position inside the poem.

loveRelationships.intro: In intimacy, freedom and honesty are non-negotiable.
body: You need authenticity, freedom, and mutual courage in connection.
When threatened, you may escalate or withdraw; when secure, you become fiercely protective and electrifyingly honest.

types.name: The Mourner
types.shortBlurb: Holds grief, memory, and tenderness with dignity.
types.subtitle: The poet who gives sorrow a voice.
types.overview: The Mourner writes with elegiac gravity. Their poems hold endings, absence, and remembrance with tenderness and control.
types.idealTagline: You know how to hold what is gone.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type may resonate with readers who love Sylvia Plath, Anne Sexton, and Elizabeth Bishop.

body: The Mourner gives language to absence without sentimentality.
Your poems honor grief as a living relationship, not a solved event.

coreTraits.intro: Elegy is your way of staying close.
body: You create space where loss can be held, named, and shared.

sections.title: What defines The Mourner
body: The Mourner is defined by elegiac attention and dignified sorrow.
This type writes to accompany loss without sentimental collapse.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Mourner poems often return to absence, aftermath, and trace-memory.
Grief is paced with care and emotional intelligence.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, sorrow becomes connective and humane.
Overextended, tone can narrow and repeat.

strengthsShadows.intro: You hold grief with unusual grace.
body: At your best, your work provides solace and recognition.
Your shadow is tonal narrowing—sorrow can eclipse other colors.

sections.title: The most prominent The Mourner strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Mourner may become tonally monochrome and trapped in elegiac repetition.
The signal is depth of feeling with limited emotional range.

sections.title: What motivates The Mourner
body: The Mourner is motivated by remembrance—giving loss shape, language, and communal meaning.
They thrive when sorrow is held alongside tenderness, gratitude, or continuation.

sections.title: How to grow as The Mourner
body: Include at least one line of present-tense life in grief-centered poems.
Vary cadence and imagery to keep elegy dynamic rather than static.

writingStyle.intro: Your cadence is patient and elegiac.
body: You often write from aftershocks, where detail becomes devotion.

sections.title: How The Mourner writes
body: The Mourner writes with patience, reverence, and sustained emotional gravity.
The style often privileges quiet over spectacle.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence is slow-breath, reflective, and gently persistent.
The voice feels tender, grave, and deeply companioning.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Include moments of present life to broaden tonal register.
Vary image fields to keep grief dynamic, not static.

loveRelationships.intro: In love, remembrance is part of devotion.
body: You carry people deeply, even across distance and time.
When burdened, you may linger in ache; when supported, you offer extraordinary tenderness, loyalty, and emotional depth.

types.name: The Storyteller
types.shortBlurb: Builds poems through scene, character, and unfolding.
types.subtitle: The poet who moves through moments.
types.overview: The Storyteller thinks in scenes. Their poems unfold through sequence, character, and emotional progression.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type might resonate with Maya Angelou, Langston Hughes, and Seamus Heaney.

overview.intro: You think in scenes and movement.
body: The Storyteller builds poems that unfold rather than merely declare.
Your strongest work carries readers through lived moments with momentum.
You trust story as a vessel for feeling and meaning.

sections.title: What defines The Storyteller
body: The Storyteller is defined by narrative momentum and scene intelligence.
Meaning unfolds through sequence, character, and turn.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Storyteller poems move with cinematic clarity and emotional pacing.
Readers are carried through events rather than abstracted summaries.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, narrative makes emotion accessible and memorable.
Overextended, explanation can crowd lyric intensity.
At your best, your poems are vivid, humane, and memorable.
Your shadow is over-explanation—plot can crowd lyric lift.

sections.title: The most prominent The Storyteller strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Storyteller can over-explain and lose lyric compression.
The signal is strong sequence but weakened poetic charge at line level.

sections.title: What motivates The Storyteller
body: The Storyteller is motivated by movement—taking readers somewhere emotionally real.
They thrive when narrative momentum and poetic texture evolve together.

sections.title: How to grow as The Storyteller
body: Cut explanatory transitions and let scene carry implication.
Revise for line music after structural drafting is complete.
You often land impact through ending reversals or final reframing.

sections.title: How The Storyteller writes
body: The Storyteller writes in scenes, beats, and transitions.
Form is often driven by unfolding event and emotional consequence.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence is propulsive and conversationally vivid.
The voice feels lived-in, clear, and reader-anchored.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Cut explanatory scaffolding that repeats what the scene already shows.
After structural edits, revise for line music and compression.

loveRelationships.intro: In relationships, shared stories become glue.
body: You bond by telling, listening, and co-creating meaningful memories.
Under stress, you may narrate instead of feel; in balance, you bring warmth, play, and deep relational continuity.

types.name: The Minimalist
types.shortBlurb: Uses restraint, silence, and precision for impact.
types.subtitle: The poet who trusts what is left unsaid.
types.overview: The Minimalist writes with compression and intent. Their poems distill feeling into clean lines, controlled space, and exact language.
types.idealTagline: You know how little a poem needs to stay.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: Readers close to this type might appreciate Bashō, Emily Dickinson, and Robert Frost.

body: The Minimalist compresses emotion into clean, durable language.
Your poems prove that silence and spacing can carry as much force as statement.
You remove until only the necessary remains.

sections.title: What defines The Minimalist
body: The Minimalist is defined by compression, restraint, and exactness.
Silence, space, and omission are active meaning-making tools.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Minimalist poems rely on pressure between what is said and unsaid.
A few lines often carry disproportionate emotional weight.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, spareness sharpens impact.
Overextended, spareness can feel inaccessible.

strengthsShadows.intro: You make silence do real work.
body: At your best, each line carries disproportionate weight.
Your shadow is emotional coolness—spareness can feel withholding.

sections.title: The most prominent The Minimalist strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Minimalist can become too sparse and emotionally opaque.
The signal is precision with insufficient entry points for readers.

sections.title: What motivates The Minimalist
body: The Minimalist is motivated by exactness—high impact with minimal excess.
They thrive when omission is intentional and still emotionally generous.

sections.title: How to grow as The Minimalist
body: Add one contextual cue per poem to improve accessibility.
Test spareness by reading aloud: if meaning collapses, restore one vital line.

writingStyle.intro: Your edits cut to the essential.
body: Your strongest poems leave room for reader participation without losing center.

sections.title: How The Minimalist writes
body: The Minimalist writes with severe precision and tonal control.
Each line is selected for necessity, not flourish.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence is clipped, intentional, and high-pressure.
The voice feels disciplined, clear, and quietly intense.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Restore one key contextual cue when meaning becomes too opaque.
Test poems aloud to ensure compression still carries emotional signal.

loveRelationships.intro: In connection, you favor clarity over spectacle.
body: You value honesty, simplicity, and emotional signal over dramatic display.
When defended, you can seem distant; when open, you offer steadiness, trust, and deeply intentional care.

types.name: The Performer
types.shortBlurb: Writes with rhythm, voice, and spoken energy.
types.subtitle: The poet who knows how a line lands out loud.
types.overview: The Performer writes for breath and impact. Their poems carry cadence, momentum, and oral presence.
types.idealTagline: You write to be heard and remembered.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: This type often shares energy with Maya Angelou, Langston Hughes, and Allen Ginsberg.

overview.intro: You write for breath, room, and impact.
body: The Performer hears the body in language: cadence, pause, strike, release.
Your poems are built to land aloud and stay remembered.

coreTraits.intro: Cadence is where your authority lives.
body: You understand performance as craft, not just charisma.

sections.title: What defines The Performer
body: The Performer is defined by spoken-energy intelligence and audience contact.
Poems are designed to land in breath, body, and memory.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Performer poems often build through repetition, escalation, and decisive line endings.
The delivery logic is audible even on silent read.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, impact and depth work together.
Overextended, impact can overpower nuance.

strengthsShadows.intro: You command a room with language.
body: At your strongest, your work electrifies and connects instantly.
Your shadow is overprojection—punch can replace depth.

sections.title: The most prominent The Performer strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Performer may over-prioritize impact and lose subtlety.
The signal is high cadence with reduced depth or modulation.

sections.title: What motivates The Performer
body: The Performer is motivated by connection in real time—language that lands in body and memory.
They thrive when rhetorical force is paired with emotional precision.

sections.title: How to grow as The Performer
body: Build quiet sections to increase contrast before climactic lines.
Replace one emphatic passage with concrete detail to deepen credibility.

writingStyle.intro: Your rhythm is built for breath and memory.
body: You often shape poems around how they move through a listener, not only a page.

sections.title: How The Performer writes
body: The Performer writes rhythm-forward with deliberate vocal architecture.
The poem is shaped around how it sounds in motion.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence is dynamic, emphatic, and stage-ready.
The voice feels charismatic, forceful, and emotionally direct.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Create quiet pockets to increase contrast and deepen listening.
Substitute one rhetorical peak with concrete imagery for balance.

loveRelationships.intro: In love, expression is generous and visible.
body: You bring enthusiasm, language, and visible devotion to relationships.
When unsteady, intensity can dominate; when grounded, you bring joy, courage, and unforgettable affirmation.

types.name: The Weaver
types.shortBlurb: Braids image, meaning, and emotion into layered work.
types.subtitle: The poet who connects everything beneath the surface.
types.overview: The Weaver creates layered coherence. Their poems braid symbol, thought, memory, and emotional tension into textured wholes.
types.idealTagline: You make complexity feel woven, not scattered.

famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.disclaimer: Illustrative creative affinities, not official classifications.
famousPoetsWithSimilarEnergy.copy: People who identify with this type may be drawn to Adrienne Rich, T. S. Eliot, and Emily Dickinson.

body: The Weaver interlaces memory, symbol, thought, and feeling across layers.
Your work rewards rereading because meaning accumulates with each pass.

coreTraits.intro: Integration is your superpower on the page.
body: You are skilled at holding contradiction without collapsing it.

sections.title: What defines The Weaver
body: The Weaver is defined by synthesis: threading image, thought, and feeling into one fabric.
Complexity is a feature, but coherence is the standard.

sections.title: How these traits appear in real writing
body: Weaver poems create interdependent meaning across repeated symbols and recalls.
Rereading typically reveals additional structural intelligence.

sections.title: Trait range: healthy vs overextended
body: At healthy range, layering feels rich and integrated.
Overextended, layering becomes dense and diffuse.
At your best, your poems feel rich without feeling random.
Your shadow is density overload—too many threads can diffuse focus.

sections.title: The most prominent The Weaver strengths
How this type handles stress
body: Under stress, The Weaver can over-layer and obscure the central thread.
The signal is rich complexity with elevated reader load.

sections.title: What motivates The Weaver
body: The Weaver is motivated by synthesis—braiding multiple meanings into one coherent fabric.
They thrive when complexity remains navigable and emotionally anchored.

sections.title: How to grow as The Weaver
body: Name the primary thread before revision and protect it relentlessly.
Reduce motif count per poem to strengthen overall coherence.

writingStyle.intro: Your motifs interlock across the whole piece.
body: You compose in networks rather than single-line statements.

sections.title: How The Weaver writes
body: The Weaver writes through motif networks and recursive structure.
Lines are often designed to converse across distance in the poem.

sections.title: Voice and line movement
body: Cadence is braided—advancing while circling key nodes.
The voice feels expansive, intelligent, and intricately held.

sections.title: Revision guidance for this style
body: Name the primary thread and cut motifs that do not serve it.
Simplify one layer per revision pass to protect readability.

loveRelationships.intro: In intimacy, you bond through nuance and pattern.
body: You connect through nuance, memory, and meaningful synthesis.
When stressed, you may over-process; when regulated, you offer profound understanding and emotionally intelligent companionship.

resultsPage.title: Your Versiq Profile

sections.name: secondaryTraits
sections.title: Adjacent Traits
sections.description: Neighboring energies that regularly appear in your writing.

sections.name: confidence
sections.title: Profile Strength
sections.description: Confidence increases with more submitted poems.

sections.name: signalSummary
sections.title: Why this result
sections.description: A concise summary of recurring signals behind your match.

sections.name: refineCta
sections.title: Refine your profile
sections.description: Submit another poem to strengthen precision over time.

resultsPage.footnote: Versiq Personalities are pattern maps, not fixed labels. Your profile can evolve as your writing evolves.

states.empty: Start with one poem. Your profile deepens over time.
states.errorShort: This text may be too short for reliable analysis.
states.errorGeneric: We couldn't analyze that poem. Please try again.

positioningLines: A personality system that reads your poems to reveal the kind of poet you are.
Submit your poems. Discover your poetic type. Refine it over time.
One poem captures a moment. A body of poems reveals a voice.
